page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the N1 server family. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 2.3 GHz with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is equipped with 208.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 6.5 GB per core. It does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate that the single-core performance of the Intel Xeon processor is relatively weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, with single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, with Geekbench multi-core, stress-ng multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression all placing in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests also show average performance, reaching 95.75 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 44.06 GB/sec for large-block write operations.

From a workload perspective, the instance is suited for memory-intensive applications that benefit from a large RAM footprint but do not require high single-threaded CPU performance. Typical use cases include database operations, caching layers like Redis, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference. The hardware profile offers a balanced resource density for legacy enterprise workloads, though the older CPU architecture represents a tradeoff in raw compute speed compared to newer generations.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-32 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs and 208.0 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It provides a dedicated CPU allocation with 16 physical cores and hyper-threading, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. While single-core benchmarks are weak, placing in the bottom 25%, its multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities perform at average levels. This instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and caching systems that require substantial memory capacity without demanding high single-threaded processing speeds.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 208 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU. It offers average multi-core performance for databases and caching.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-32 features 32 vCPUs, 208 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server designed for database operations, caching, and multi-threaded workloads requiring substantial RAM capacity.

Powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon processor

No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for databases, caching, and memory-intensive applications
